The search didn't return directly relevant kinetics papers, but the underlying principle (saturating reaction-order dependence on monomer concentration via Michaelis-Menten/Hill kinetics) is well established in the Knowles-Cohen amyloid master equation framework, where secondary nucleation saturates at high monomer concentration. This supports the breakthrough idea: a bounded saturating modulation of the rate by x1 rather than an unbounded linear factor.

I'll implement the breakthrough idea exactly: a saturating Hill/Michaelis-Menten modulation factor `1 + c2*x1/(c3+x1)`, with the guarded positive denominator. This reduces to the current form when c2≈0, giving a safe multi-start anchor, and stays smooth/bounded over normalized x1∈[0,1].

- **Parsimony:** Only one added constant and one division; complexity stays low (well under the limit), so the parsimony penalty change is minimal while the structural payoff is concrete.

This is the minimal change implementing the breakthrough idea, with a graceful reduction to the proven parent to keep robustness across all 24 datasets.
